action: Returns from the afternoon forage with Ryan and Jade; takes position at the firepit to cook the fish over hot flames, movements deliberate and grounded.
thought: She asked if I'd stay. I meant it.

action: Leads the foraging team back to camp with fish and foraged greens; begins organizing food preparation at the firepit, already thinking about tomorrow's water search.
thought: Everything depends on them knowing I'm here.

action: Returns from the afternoon forage hungry and thirsty; sits at the firepit watching the fish cook, then asks the questions she's been holding since yesterday.
thought: I'm terrified they'll wake up tomorrow and leave.

action: Watches from the house porch as the three return with food, her hand resting on the stray's head, visible relief crossing her face.
thought: They came back together. They're still here.

action: Remains close to Judy on the porch, ears perking up as the smell of cooking fish drifts across the camp, tail beginning to wag with hope.
thought: She is safe. Food is coming.

description: With fish from the afternoon forage sizzling over the firepit, Marcus, Ryan, and Jade work side by side; Jade asks what they're all afraid of, and both men answer her honestly for the first time.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The trap caught three. That's better than yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Greens are wilting. Need to cook soon.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm starving. I can barely feel my feet.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You did good today. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We did what needed doing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did anyone check the south path? I thought I saw more palms there.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should go back tomorrow. The mushrooms there—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water's the priority. Everything else comes after.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fair. But we need variety, or people get sick.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: My throat is killing me. Can we please eat?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. That's why we're here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Found a seepage mark near the boulder watch. Might be a source.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You found that today and didn't say anything?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Didn't want to promise what wasn't sure.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well, let's explore it tomorrow—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can we just eat first?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Right. Sorry.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll tend the fire. Fish needs heat.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: She looks better, doesn't she? Judy.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: She does. Last night helped, I think.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The promises did. She needed to know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you ever worry they'll change? Tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: All the time.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not about staying. I'm sure of that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How are you sure?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because I chose. That's all that matters.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's good. But Jade's right to worry—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not asking for reassurance. I'm asking if you're scared too.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. I wake up every morning afraid something's broken.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It hasn't. Won't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't promise that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. But I can promise I won't run.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The fish is ready when you are, Judy!

action: He sits back from the fire, watching Jade and Ryan speak, his responses spare but honest, telling them what solitude cost him.
thought: Solitude was safer than this. But this is worth it.

action: He sits close to Judy at the fire's edge, taking her hand as the conversation deepens, promising to stay despite his own fear.
thought: I can't leave them, even though I wake afraid.

action: She asks the hardest questions—what scared means, what staying actually means—and listens as both men lay themselves open.
thought: They're actually here. They're choosing this.

action: She sits at the fire with the stray pressed against her leg, listening to the men's confessions, and finally allows herself to believe they meant their promises.
thought: Maybe they really will stay. I can rest.

action: It moves between the group, whimpering softly at tension, pressing its whole body against Judy's leg, sensing her relief as the evening settles.
thought: She is safe now. We are safe together.

description: Jade pushed them to speak their deepest fears tonight—Ryan and Marcus answered with brutal honesty, Judy finally believed them, and the stray pressed close, sensing everything shift.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: When you say you're scared, do you mean it's not real—or it is?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Both. I wake up convinced I dreamed this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's real.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Real but fragile. We're all holding still, afraid to break it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because if it breaks—

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: We're alone again. And I've been alone. I can't go back.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Whimpers, shifts closer to Judy; she rests a hand on its head.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You won't.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You can't promise that. People leave. That's what they do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not people. I'm here. I chose here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: So did I.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But we're terrified. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fear doesn't mean we're leaving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It means we're human. And humans do leave.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Stands, presses its whole body against Judy's leg; she pulls it close.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not all of them.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You used to be alone by choice.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I was. It felt safe until I realized how much it cost.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: How do you stop being afraid it'll happen again?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: A long pause; Ryan reaches over, takes Judy's free hand.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't. You just stay anyway.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Every day. The way the pup does.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It doesn't even know to be afraid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Neither did we, once.

action: He sits back from the fire, watching the others in the glow, his hand resting on the pup's neck.
thought: I told them everything. They're still here.

action: He stares into the flames, jaw tight, fingers picking at a crack in the wood beside him.
thought: Did I say too much? Will they leave?

action: She wraps her arms around her knees, tilted toward Ryan and Marcus, her gaze flickering between them.
thought: They meant it. So why do I doubt?

action: She settles on the porch steps with the pup at her side, her hand on her belly, breathing slowly.
thought: They promised. I heard them. I believe now.

action: It rests its long body against Judy's leg, one ear twitched toward the fire, content in the quiet.
thought: She is safe. We are safe.

description: Late night by the fire, Ryan breaks the silence with a question clawing at him: the hunger, the thirst, the weight of what they just promised each other.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. I keep thinking about what I said earlier, and now I'm just... what if we can't actually do this?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You mean the promises?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I mean all of it. We're not eating, water's gone, and in three days we're going to be desperate.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We've been desperate since day one.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, but then we weren't depending on each other to stay sane. Now...

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Now if one of us breaks, we all break.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not breaking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You might not have a choice.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: There's always a choice. I chose to stay. I'll choose it again tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But what if I can't? What if the fear just gets worse?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we hold you up. Like you held me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And if you start falling too?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll hold you both.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't carry all of us, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Probably not. But I'm going to try anyway.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the scariest thing you've said all day.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why is it scary?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because if you fail, I'm going to believe it's my fault for needing you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's not how it works.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How does it work, then?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You just show up. You don't owe anyone perfect. Just present.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: When did you become the wise one?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: When you made me admit I'm terrified every morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're all terrified.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. And somehow that makes it better.

action: By dawn he was sitting by his tent when Jade found him, and they talked through the morning light.
thought: I meant every word I told them.

action: He woke early and moved through camp checking the fire and supplies, trying to shake off his anxiety.
thought: Are they really here, or just staying for now?

action: She left her hammock at first light and walked toward the cliff area, drawn to find Marcus waiting.
thought: They meant it. We're actually doing this.

action: She woke to the stray pressed against her side and stayed in bed, letting herself rest for the first time.
thought: They kept their promise. I'm finally safe.

action: The pup woke beside Judy, pressed close as she stirred, completely content in the early morning quiet.
thought: She's here. We are safe now.

description: In early light by the cliff, Marcus sits with last night's weight—until Jade finds him and they speak the quiet truth they both needed to hear.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You didn't sleep.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Couldn't. Kept going over what I said last night.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it. Every word.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. I could hear it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's different in the morning. More real, more terrifying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you're still here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: So are you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: When it gets hard, are you going to run?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm going to try not to. That's all I can promise right now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's all I'm doing too. Just choosing to stay today, and we'll choose again tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then we'll do it together.

action: He walks down from his tent at midday and sits with them at the firepit, despite every instinct telling him to run to the cliff.
thought: I'm terrified and staying anyway.

action: He tends the fire at midday, adding wood and checking the embers, then sits down as Marcus arrives.
thought: Is he really here? Is this real?

action: She drifts over to the fire and settles on the ground, watching Marcus the moment he arrives, eyes warm.
thought: He came down. He's really choosing this.

action: She moves carefully from the house porch toward the firepit, resting most of her weight on a palm trunk, but drawn by the voices.
thought: They're all here. They're not leaving.

action: The pup watches from the edge of the firepit clearing, ears forward, tail uncertain but present, as all four gather together.
thought: They are here. We are safe.

description: At midday, Marcus finally sits with them instead of the cliff — Jade watching, Ryan tending fire, Judy present — and he speaks the truth he's been holding: he's terrified but choosing to stay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I wasn't sure you'd come down.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Neither was I. I almost didn't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you did.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I did.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because of last night?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because of last night. And this morning. And the fact that Jade was right.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: About the choice being real. Being mine. Not just happening to me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So you're choosing this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm choosing today. Right now. I'm choosing to sit here with you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That matters. More than you know.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Does it matter enough that you'll stay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yes. It does.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The pup's been watching us the whole time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not leaving either.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just like us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because it's safe now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I want it to be. I want it to stay safe. Even when I'm afraid.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You said that. Last night. And this morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I did. And I meant it. I still mean it, even when I want to run.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But you're not running.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. I'm sitting here instead. Terrified. But staying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Staying for what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For you. All of you. For this feeling that maybe I don't have to be alone.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You're not alone.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. I'm starting to know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's enough for today?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: More than enough. For today.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tomorrow I'll choose again.

action: He drifted toward the cliff in early afternoon, doubt creeping back despite his promises, until Jade found him and pulled him back into the real world.
thought: Doubt still comes. But I'm staying anyway.

action: He moved restlessly between the firepit and the house, tallying empty stores of food and water, worry tightening his chest each time he counted.
thought: How long can we go without eating?

action: She sensed Marcus's withdrawal and went after him, finding him at the cliff's edge and refusing to let him disappear into his own head again.
thought: I won't let him run. Not now.

action: She sat on the house porch in the shade, the baby pressing heavy against her ribs, and tried not to think about hunger or what would happen if they ran out.
thought: Will they stay when the hunger comes?

action: The pup pressed against Judy's legs on the porch, sensing her anxiety, and stayed close as the afternoon heat pressed down on the camp.
thought: She is scared. I will stay.

description: As the afternoon heat presses down and hunger starts to gnaw, Jade finds Marcus withdrawing toward the cliff again — and presses him to remember what he promised this morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're leaving us again. I can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. I'm just... thinking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About what? About how hard this is? About us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: About whether I can actually stay. When it's this hard.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can you?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't know yet. But I'm going to try.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not the same as staying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's all I have right now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then let me help. You don't have to do this alone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's the part I'm not used to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. But you're staying anyway — that's what counts.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it this morning. I still mean it now. Even when I doubt.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Good. Because we're not losing you to the cliff again.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know.

action: He sits at the fire as evening deepens, hungry and thirsty but present, his choice to stay being tested by survival.
thought: Staying is harder than I thought. But I'm here.

action: He keeps the fire burning, anxiously checking the empty water pitcher and trying to calculate how they'll feed themselves tomorrow.
thought: I should have pushed harder. I failed them.

action: She returns from the tide pools at dusk with water cupped in her hands, her calm decisive action answering the day's question.
thought: We found water. Tomorrow we fish properly.

action: She sits close to Marcus as darkness falls, the water from Jade's hands finally easing her thirst and her terror.
thought: He stayed. He meant it. I believe him.

action: The puppy presses hard against Judy's leg, trembling with hunger and fear, then slowly stills as she pulls it close.
thought: She was scared. I stayed. We're safe together.

description: As evening falls and hunger gnaws sharp, the group gathers at the fire and realizes no one foraged—Jade crosses to the tide pools for water while Marcus sits steady with Judy, proving his morning choice is real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The water's gone. So's the food. How did we let this happen?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We were here. That was the priority today.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But I'm so thirsty. And the baby—what if we can't—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We get water. We do it the way we chose this morning.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should have foraged. I should have pushed harder.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'll go to the tide pools. I know where the water collects. Twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You're leaving? You're really leaving us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm staying. You're not doing this alone.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'll keep the fire hot. Jade will be back before dark.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. I know I'm being needy. I know you said—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're eight months pregnant and scared. That's not needy. That's sense.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm going now. Stay here. I'll be fast.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're right here, Judy. We're not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Neither am I.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Found water at the tide pools. It's brackish but it's water.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Thank god. Judy, drink this slowly.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Oh. Oh, thank you. Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You keep apologizing for being human. Stop.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we fish. We take it seriously. We build a trap.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The fire stays hot. We have enough wood. We'll make it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Will you really? Even when it gets worse? Will you stay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I promised. I chose this morning. I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're all staying. We meant it and we mean it now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Even hungry. Even scared. We're doing this.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay. I believe you. I think I can sleep now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Good. We'll keep watch.

action: Sits at the fire and names his fear — wanting to leave — before choosing to stay.
thought: They heard me. I'm choosing to stay.

action: Tends the fire and listens as Marcus speaks his truth, anxiety easing into trust.
thought: He's staying. We're all staying.

action: Sits by Marcus at the fire, witnessing his vulnerability, steadying the moment as he names his choice.
thought: He doesn't have to be alone anymore.

action: Settles beside Ryan and holds the stray close, finally believing the promises won't break.
thought: They meant it. We're actually safe.

action: Pads from the darkness into the firelit circle, pressing close to Judy, finally finding safety.
thought: Safe. I'm safe with them.

description: Marcus speaks his fear at the firepit — naming the cliff, naming the hunger to leave — and chooses to stay while the group gathers close and the stray drifts into the warmth.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I keep thinking we should have been fishing all day. I let you all down.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You didn't. You were here. You stayed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But we're hungry. Really hungry now. What if—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're still together. That's what matters.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is it? When it gets worse, will you all still—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I thought about leaving. This afternoon. At the cliff.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. I went after you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I was ready to go. To stop feeling all of this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Don't say that. Please don't—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm just being honest. I was done. Then I realized something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't want to be done. Not if it means losing you.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: So you're staying? You promise?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm choosing to stay. Every day, I'll choose again.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And we're choosing you. All of us. That has to be enough.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's enough because we choose it. Together.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Even when we're starving? Even then?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Even then. That's when it matters most.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [whimper, pads over from the shadows, presses against Marcus]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: See? Everything stays. The fire, the pup, us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come here, boy. You're safe.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: We're all safe now. Because we decided we are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Rest soon. Tomorrow we try again.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Sleep soon. The fire will keep warm.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. Together. That's the plan.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And we have water now. Jade found it. That's something real.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We keep finding small things. The fire, the shelter, each other.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we'll find more. Right?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we'll find more. Judy's right.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: And you'll be here? All of you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: All of us. Always.

action: He wakes sick and finds Jade waiting at his tent—they talk about forever, then head together to the tide pools for water.
thought: Today I can stay. Tomorrow, I don't know.

action: He wakes in the house and realizes Marcus and Jade are gone—hunger gnaws at him as he waits on the porch.
thought: Are they okay? Did I mess this up?

action: She finds Marcus emerging from his tent at dawn and asks him the question she's been afraid to ask—will he stay when it gets harder?
thought: Is his answer enough? I'm not sure yet.

action: She wakes in the house and realizes how quiet it is—no voices, no footsteps—just her and the pup breathing nearby.
thought: Will they come back to me?

action: It wakes nestled against Judy's side and stretches into the morning, its ears alert but its body calm and trusting.
thought: She's here. Safe. We're still together.

description: Jade finds Marcus at dawn and asks if his promise lasts beyond today; his honest answer sends them both to the tide pools for water.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You awake? The camp is dry, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. Give me a moment.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're sick. How are you even going to help?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm going. You're not dragging water alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Last night you said you'd stay. Every day, you said. Every day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did you? Because tomorrow we're still hungry. The day after, we're still tired. We might never be full, never be safe.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So why stay? Why choose us if it's always going to be this hard?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because you're here. Because Ryan keeps building like we're staying forever. Because that pup fell asleep against Judy without flinching.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not an answer, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's the only one I have. I'm here today. Tomorrow I'll choose again. Beyond that—I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't promise me anything.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. But I'm standing right here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's enough. For now, that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then let's make sure today has water.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Let's go.

action: He walks with Ryan to the tide pools, gathers water, and returns to sit at the fire, steady but exhausted.
thought: I keep them safe, even like this.

action: He pushes himself to the tide pools despite the sickness, gathers water, and returns to collapse by the fire.
thought: They need me to be strong right now.

action: She follows them to the tide pools, watches the struggle, and returns to the fire with doubt blooming in her chest.
thought: Can they really stay when everything's this hard?

action: She waits at camp with the stray, watching the path and fighting against the fear that they won't come back.
thought: Will they come back? Will they stay?

action: The pup stays close to Judy, sensing her anxiety and pressing against her legs whenever she looks toward the path.
thought: Something's wrong. They're all scared.

description: At the tide pools, Ryan pushes to gather water while Marcus insists they go together; Jade watches them both and wonders if love survives this kind of hunger.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The tide pools are maybe twenty minutes away. I can be there and back by afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You have no energy. Neither do I. Neither does Jade.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I have some. Not much.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then you stay. I go.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You go and collapse. Then someone has to carry you back.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I won't collapse. I'm fine.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Your hands are shaking.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: From hunger. Not weakness.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Both of you have a point. We need water badly. But he's right—you're sick.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So are you, practically. So is Marcus. We're all running on nothing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Which is exactly why we don't push. We go slow. We go together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Slower means using more energy over time. Faster we get back sooner.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Faster you pass out halfway. Then what?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Maybe he comes with you, like he said. The three of us go together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't want to drag either of you down with me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not dragging anyone. I'm going because it's what we do.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We stay together. That's what you said at the fire.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay. You're right. I just... I need to feel useful. I need to do something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You do matter. We know that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You are useful. You're here. That's enough.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is it enough? After everything you said last night?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He meant it. We all did.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it. I still mean it. Even when I'm scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you said you don't know about tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't. But today I'm choosing to stay. That's all I have to give.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that actually enough for all of us?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It has to be. Let's go get the water.

action: He retreated to the meadow rocks after the tide pools, wrestling with the fear that the group would splinter if he stopped holding so tight.
thought: I can't promise tomorrow, only that today I choose.

action: He worked on reinforcing the firepit frame with leftover wood, filling the afternoon silence with deliberate, careful labor.
thought: Did I push too hard this morning?

action: Mid-afternoon she slipped away from camp and found Marcus alone at the meadow rocks, needing to understand what fear she'd seen him carry back from the pools.
thought: Why does everything depend on him holding on?

action: She stayed near the house porch all afternoon with the pup pressed against her side, watching the empty paths and worrying about invisible fractures.
thought: Will they come back? Will they stay with me?

action: It stayed pressed against Judy's side all afternoon, sensing the group's strain but anchored by the steady rise and fall of her breathing.
thought: She's scared but I'm here. We're safe.

description: By afternoon, Jade finds Marcus sitting alone at the meadow rocks, unable to ignore the fear she watched settle into him at the tide pools this morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You've been alone all afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Thinking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About this morning? About Ryan?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: About whether I can actually hold us together.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why do you feel like you have to?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because I promised. Because I'm terrified that if I don't, you'll all drift.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We won't leave because you let go.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You can't promise that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Neither can you, but you keep trying anyway.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. I hate it. But I can't stop myself.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if you did? What if you just let us choose?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What if you all choose to leave?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then I leave. But that doesn't mean I'm leaving because you failed.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I promised to keep you safe.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You do. But not by holding us captive. By being here, day after day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What if one day I can't be here anymore?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we figure it out. But I believe you when you say you want to.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I do. More than anything.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then that's your promise. Not forever—just today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: And tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Tomorrow you choose again. That's all anyone has.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Every day?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Every day. That's the only kind of love that survives this.

action: Marcus walks back from the meadow rocks with Jade, settles at the firepit as evening deepens, and quietly tends the fire with steady hands despite his exhaustion.
thought: Today I'm choosing. That's all I have.

action: Ryan heads out to find Marcus and Jade, finds them returning from the rocks, and gathers the group at the firepit as he settles beside Marcus.
thought: He won't promise forever, so I choose today.

action: Jade returns with Marcus to the firepit as evening settles, sits near the fire, and watches the group learn to hold just today instead of tomorrow.
thought: They're learning. It's hard but they're learning.

action: Judy waits at the firepit with Stray through the afternoon and evening, holds the puppy close as Marcus returns, and tries to understand what his words mean for tomorrow.
thought: Just today. Maybe I can hold just today.

action: The puppy stays close to Judy at the firepit through the evening, pressed against her as the group gathers, offering steady warmth and presence.
thought: She's scared. I stay. We are safe.

description: As evening fell, Marcus and Jade returned from the rocks to the firepit, and the weight of Marcus's words settled in: he could only promise today.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You've been gone a while. Everything okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just needed to think.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We talked. About hard things.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Like what? Is something wrong?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. Just... real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Real is good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I can only promise today. Not tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: What does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It means he's choosing us right now. That's all any of us can do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I can do that. Choose today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Even when it's hard?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even then.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: So what if tomorrow you change your mind?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then I'd leave. But I'm not going to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can't promise what's not yours to control.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Right.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Today is a long word.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It is. But it's enough.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: *whimpers softly, presses harder against Judy*

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay, today.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're all here. Right now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire needs wood. It's getting cold.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'll help.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Just today. We can do that.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: *tail moves, settles against Judy's side in the warmth*

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'll get fruit. It should be eaten soon.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Good.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're okay. We're going to be okay.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Today we are.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Today we are.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: *curls fully against Judy, content and safe*

action: After naming his fear at the firepit this morning, Marcus sits alone by the dimming fire at nightfall, staring into the embers as if the answers might be there.
thought: Today I choose. That's all I have.

action: Ryan sits across the firepit from Marcus, close enough to hear every word, his anxiety slowly softening as he listens to the conversation unfold.
thought: He's here now. That has to matter.

action: Jade walks over from the shadows and settles beside Marcus by the dying fire, her presence a quiet question waiting to be spoken.
thought: Will he choose us tomorrow too?

action: Judy settles down nearby with the stray pressed against her leg, too tired and too scared to retreat to the house alone tonight.
thought: They promised. Will they keep choosing?

action: The stray lies against Judy's leg, steady and warm, sensing her fear but offering wordless comfort through simple, loyal presence.
thought: She's scared. I stay. We're safe.

description: After Marcus named his fear at the firepit this morning, he sits silent by the dying fire at nightfall—and Jade finally asks him the question he's been avoiding: Can this actually last?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're quiet tonight.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can this actually last? Like, between all of us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not what I asked.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Today, it lasts. Tomorrow, I'll choose again. That's all I have.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't have to figure it out tonight, man.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not. I'm just saying what's true.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Will you stay when it gets harder? When you want to leave?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: When I want to leave, I've been running to you instead now. That's a choice I keep making.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's enough for today.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is it though?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I need it to be. Please.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. I'm trying. Every day I'm trying.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We all are. That's the thing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not asking because I don't believe you. I'm asking so you know what scares me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What scares you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That you'll wake up one day and realize this is too hard. That you'll choose to leave.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I could. But right now, I'm choosing to stay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then we rest tonight. Together.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The stray's scared too.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're all scared. But we're here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. We're here.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the promise.

action: Woke tense from yesterday's promise, heard Ryan moving nearby, and decided to face the morning instead of running.
thought: Can I choose again? Just today.

action: Rose before dawn, gathered the fishing poles, and found Marcus coming out of his tent to meet the day.
thought: He's still here. That's what matters.

action: Woke in her hammock, heard the two of them heading to the water, and set out alone to gather firewood and think.
thought: He really meant it. He's choosing.

action: Moved slowly from the house into the morning light, the stray close beside her, heading to tend the fire.
thought: Everyone's safe. We're all okay today.

action: Followed Judy from her sleeping place to the firepit, pressed against her leg as the day warmed, certain and calm.
thought: She is here. We are safe.

description: Marcus wakes raw from yesterday's naming, but Ryan silently brings fishing poles, and they wade out together—proving that choosing today is real, not just words said in firelight.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're up early.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Couldn't sleep.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on, then. Fish won't catch themselves.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: *picks up the pole* Where?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Reef edge. But first—I heard you last night, at the fire.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know you did.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm glad you talked. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: *starts walking toward the beach* Jade asked if it could last. I couldn't lie to her.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What did you say?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That I don't know tomorrow. Only today.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And today?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: *pauses at the shore* Today I'm here. With you. Choosing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: *smiles, wades in* Then let's fish.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: *follows*

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The tide's right. We might get lucky.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We usually do, don't we.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because we're good at this. Together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: *casts the net* Yeah. We are.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's enough, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For now. For today. Yeah.

action: He works alongside Ryan at the firepit, resetting stones with methodical care, Jade's presence steadying something inside him that threatened to crack at dawn.
thought: Can I choose again tomorrow, or is today all I have?

action: He shows Marcus how to place the stones, moving unhurried and warm, letting Marcus see that choosing today is real because they're choosing it together.
thought: He's holding it together. I can see it working.

action: She drifts over to the firepit midway through the morning, settling across from Marcus and Ryan, watching him hold the daylight the way he held it in darkness.
thought: He's here, choosing daylight over darkness now.

action: She sits on the house porch with water, watching the three of them work, the stray pressed against her leg, everything stable for the first time in weeks.

action: It follows the humans briefly to the firepit, then settles against Judy on the porch, its body warm and solid against her leg.

description: Marcus and Ryan repair the firepit while Jade drifts close, watching the choice he made this morning hold firm in daylight.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on, help me reset these stones. We can fix this before the real heat hits.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah, okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How are you holding up, really?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Exhausted. But still here.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's what matters.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Room for one more?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You don't have to be here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I want to. What needs doing?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Base stones are loose. We need them tight or the whole thing shifts.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Like everything else—you build the foundation right, it holds.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What if the foundation breaks?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then you fix it. Or you build again.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you don't give up on the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You make it sound so simple.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: My grandmother built walls her whole life. She used to say the small stones are what hold it all up.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Is she still building?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: She died years ago. But I saw her walls last year—they're still standing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Someone kept showing up for them.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: How often?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Every spring, maybe. Someone from the family. Not all the time, just when it mattered.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's how we do this. We show up. Not perfectly—just when it matters.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm terrified I'll forget how to stay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we'll remind you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on, one more section. Then we're done.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: And then what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Lunch. Rest. Tomorrow we do it again.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Simple as that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Nothing's simple.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But this is. Just choosing again tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: One day at a time. That's all any of us can promise.

action: He works beside Marcus in steady rhythm, hands busy, anchoring him in daylight.

action: She drifts to the firepit to watch Marcus work, drawn by his choice holding firm.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hand me that stone, yeah? We're almost done with the edge here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What are we actually building?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A place to sit. For all of us. Like a real bench.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We don't need—

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know we don't need it. I want it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is this the grand expansion?

dialogue.line: Come help. Tell Marcus this is a good idea.

dialogue.line: I like watching you two work. You're... synchronized.

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why? You're good together. He steadies you.

dialogue.line: I can be louder. But you listen when it matters.

dialogue.line: After yesterday, I wasn't sure you'd stay in daylight too.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Neither was I.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you came fishing. You chose him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He chose us. Plural. That's the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: One day at a time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's enough. That's more than most people get.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Help us move this rock. It's heavier than I thought.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course. Where's it going?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Right there. That corner. Marcus, together?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Same as this morning. Ready?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: On three?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: One... two... three.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's settling. That's good.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're all a thing now. A real thing, aren't you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're trying. Right, Marcus?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're here. That's all I can promise.

dialogue.line: Fire's burning good tonight. How's your hunger?

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.line: And now we're carrying it with you.

dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's how we do it. I see how it should be, you make it real.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You balance each other.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're just trying to keep standing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then we stand together. That's what we do now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: How are you so sure?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not. But I'm here. And you're here. And that's enough for tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Tomorrow you choose again.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And I'll be here when you do.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.line: Said I would.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. I know you did. I just... I needed to see it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not good at this. The staying. You know that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's why it matters. That you chose to anyway.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm still scared.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Of what? Of us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Of wanting this much. Of it not being enough.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It is. You are. You're enough for me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Morning's different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. But you're still here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I still choose this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Every day?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Trying to. One at a time.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all I'm asking for.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Morning. You two look serious.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I wasn't worried. Not really.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You should be. This is hard.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Everything real is hard. That's how you know it's real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: See? That's why I love her.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know why you do.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can we eat? The fire's good. We could cook something.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We could. Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You'll stay for it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll stay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even the puppy knows. You're part of this now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm part of this now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then let's build something. Not a structure. A day. A real day together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on then. Let's cook.

action: He steadied posts while Ryan hammered, moving with quiet certainty that surprised them both.
thought: This is what choosing feels like, I think.

action: He worked on reinforcing the porch beam, testing each section with his hands to feel for weakness.
thought: He's still here, I can actually believe him.

action: She brought supplies from the garden, pausing to watch the quiet synchronization between Marcus and Ryan.
thought: Something shifted when I stopped running.

action: She rested on the porch cushions, hands on her belly, watching the three of them work with something like awe.
thought: They're really here, really choosing me.

action: The puppy curled up near the work, calm and present, its breathing matched the rhythm of the hammer blows.
thought: All here, all working, all with me.

description: Ryan works on the porch while Marcus steadies the posts, Jade brings supplies and Judy rests and watches; the puppy is calm and curled up nearby, no longer waiting.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The weathering's worse than I thought. We need to replace this beam.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll help.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Could take two hours.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then we take the time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You two move like you've built together forever.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Do we? I feel like I'm making it up.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not making anything up. You're steady.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's what I mean. You ground each other.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is that what this is? Grounding?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. That's what it is.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The pup knows. Look how settled it is.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It learned to stay when we proved we'd stay.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: And you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I learned the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That staying is a choice you make again every morning?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Is it easy?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No. It's terrifying sometimes. But I'd be more terrified if any of you left.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So we're all choosing the fear of losing each other.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better than the fear of being alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not alone anymore. That's new for me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: None of us are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're not.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look at you two. You actually mean that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I do. Every word.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's real. This is actually real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's solid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: All of it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then let's stop being afraid of it.

action: Sits by the firepit with the puppy settling against him, anchored and still.
thought: He trusts me. I can feel that now.

action: Leaves his work at the house porch to join Marcus and Jade by the fire.
thought: He's actually letting us need him.

action: Brings firewood from the pile and settles near Marcus, watching the puppy's trust deepen.
thought: Everything we fought for is holding.

action: Rests in the house and watches the group by the fire, finally able to be still.
thought: I can sleep now. I'm safe.

action: Emerges from the palms and walks directly to Marcus, pressing close without hesitation or fear.
thought: He's safe. We are safe.

description: The puppy settles against Marcus by the firepit without fear, and Jade and Ryan gather close to watch this small miracle.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's been following you since morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He knows how to be with me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You've really got him now. He won't even come to me anymore.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's different. He just trusts it will be okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's what we all had to learn. That you would be okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hard-won, too. You fought us pretty hard on it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Didn't think I was worth the fight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He doesn't have that doubt. He just knows you won't hurt him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe that's why he needed you. Because you're learning the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What thing?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That people won't leave. That staying is a choice, not a trap.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm still terrified, though. Some days more than others.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We know. You wake up scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you're still here. And that's the whole thing, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. And you're there too. That matters more than the fear.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's love, isn't it? Choosing it even when you're terrified.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're teaching him that. Not by talking. By showing up.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're going to be alright, you know. We're not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: None of us are.

action: Settled at the firepit's edge as the sun went gold, still feeling Ryan's shoulder-press from the afternoon's work sinking into his bones.
thought: They're choosing me. Every day they choose.

action: Crossed from the porch to the circle as dusk fell, hands callused, chest light in a way it had never been before.
thought: This is what being home actually feels like.

action: Drifted to the fire and settled without thinking, close enough to Marcus that their shoulders nearly touched, no part of her running anymore.
thought: I'm staying. Not someday. Now. Really.

action: Rested in the circle's warmth, one hand on her belly, watching the three of them breathe, finally believing they would not disappear.
thought: They stayed. They're still staying.

action: Pressed against Marcus's leg without tension, breathing steady, no longer tensed for the moment the world would shatter.
thought: All here. All safe. All chosen.

description: After a day of building together, all five rest at the firepit—Marcus and Ryan tired but secure, Jade beside Marcus, Judy calm, the stray still.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Those posts are going to hold for years. We built something solid today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We did. Didn't know you had it in you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Was that a compliment, or just an observation?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The sun's hitting the water just right. Everything's turning gold.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Like we planned it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We didn't plan anything. That's what makes it feel real.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I was watching you both work. The way you move together now—it's like you've always done this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: First time building with someone I actually trust like this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: First time for all of us. I didn't know I could feel like this.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is it always going to feel this safe?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. Sometimes it'll be harder, but we'll stay anyway. That's what choosing means.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Every morning, if I have to. Every single day.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I was afraid of staying because I had to. But this is different.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: How is it different?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because I want to. Because waking up next to him means everything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even the pup knows it. He's not waiting for the world to break.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He learned we stay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Should we give him a name?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not yet. Let him show us who he is first.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: How long will that take?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: However long it takes. That's what staying means.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I believe that now. I'm learning to believe that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: None of us are. We're choosing to be here, every day.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The baby's moving. I think the baby can feel this safety.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Of course it can. All of this is for the people we love.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I never thought I'd feel this. After the crash, after everything...

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're safe now. We all are.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No one's taking that from us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I believe that. Finally, I believe that.

action: He lets the stray settle wholly against his chest while the fire burns low, and for once he doesn't wait for the moment to shatter.
thought: They're here. They're staying. Maybe it's real.

action: He tends the fire with quiet purpose, watching Marcus hold the stray, finally believing in what he came hoping to find.
thought: He actually believes we'll stay now. It's happening.

action: She settles beside Marcus as the firelight dims, close enough to feel the warmth of him and the stray, and stops running even inside her head.
thought: We're actually here. All of us. We're really staying.

action: She lies in the house, breathing deepening into sleep so restful it feels like being reborn, knowing all four are outside keeping vigil.
thought: They promised. They stayed. I can finally rest.

action: The puppy breathes steadily against Marcus's chest, its body completely relaxed, no flinch even in sleep—it has learned that this heartbeat means safety.
thought: He holds me. This is home. Safe.

description: As night falls at the firepit, Marcus lets the stray settle wholly against him while Ryan tends the flames and Jade watches from close beside—three quiet figures believing, for the first time, that this fragile thing might hold.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He doesn't even twitch anymore. Just fully settles against you like he knows for certain he's safe.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah, he does.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because of you, though. That's the thing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because all of us stayed. Not just me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay, but you're the one he actually bonded with most.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's never going to accept a compliment, Ryan. Just watch him deflect.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm aware of my issues.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's so Marcus. You know exactly what's wrong with you?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Pretty much.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's different though. Less like he's waiting for everyone to leave.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is that something you felt? With us, before?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. That you might.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And now?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Now I wait for it, but less. You keep being here and it doesn't break.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe it won't. Ever.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm starting to think maybe.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you think he remembers being afraid?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Probably. But he's not anymore.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Are you still afraid, Marcus? Really?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I wake up afraid most mornings. But less so.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What changed?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You all keep waking up and staying. So I wake up less afraid.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Are you coming in soon?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, soon. Just sitting with them.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Thank you for today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sleep, Judy. We're here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You meant that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the shift. You actually believing we're staying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Him knowing we won't leave.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm learning to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're doing it.

action: Wakes with the stray against him, the fire still warm, and tends the embers before anyone else stirs.
thought: They're still here. They chose me.

action: Rises from the house at first light, drawn by the smell of the fire and the voices beginning to gather.
thought: Waking up here is different. Better.

action: Leaves the hammock when she hears them, moving toward the firepit and the easy pull of all of them together.
thought: I'm actually staying. Not just planning to.

action: Wakes inside the house, the baby moving, and rests there listening to the voices at the firepit—content to be safe while they tend the morning.
thought: Safe. They stayed. They're still staying.

action: Stretches against Marcus's leg at first light, tail moving, entirely certain that the world wakes with the five of them together.
thought: He's here. I'm here. We're safe.

description: Early morning at the firepit, all four gathering as naturally as breathing, ready to name tonight what they've been quietly choosing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I woke up happy. Is that allowed? Just... happy.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why wouldn't it be?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because good things don't usually last. But this is.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Morning's not over yet.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Still waiting for it to break. That's Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Aware, not waiting.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's actually growth. Old Marcus would never say that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Old Marcus had less to lose.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What does he want?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He's got what he wants. Just likes reminding us he's here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should do that. Remind each other. Say it out loud.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Say what?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That we're staying. That we choose each other. That it's real.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I believe that now. Really believe it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So do I.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then say it tonight. At the fire. All of us together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even the scared parts?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Especially those. That's what makes it real.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: All in, then.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: All in.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: All in.

action: Marcus arrives at the garden midday with palm fronds, finding Ryan sitting anxiously with the stray, and stays to listen.
thought: He needed to hear it again. I'll tell him as many times as it takes.

action: Ryan sits in the garden talking to the stray, waiting for something to be wrong; when Marcus arrives, he finally speaks what he's been holding.
thought: He chose me. Every time. I can actually rest now.

action: Jade drifts to the palm shade after the morning work, watching the camp settle into its rhythm and feeling the weight of yesterday lift.
thought: This is what staying feels like. It's real.

action: Judy settles on the house porch, resting her back against the wall, watching Ryan and Marcus in the distance with the stray and feeling safe in their nearness.
thought: They're still here. Everyone is still here.

action: The stray naps in the garden between Ryan and Marcus, pressed against both their legs, breathing out the last of its worry.
thought: Safe. Both here. We are safe.

description: In the garden midday, Marcus finds Ryan sitting anxiously with the stray and stays to listen—and through the work of building a shelter, Ryan finally speaks his fear of being secondary and Marcus answers him plainly.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You know what, I think you're actually the smartest one here. You get fed, you get rest, nobody asks you to worry about anything.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: (lifts head, ear perks, then settles back down)

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, I see you listening. You're faking sleep, aren't you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's been awake the whole time. (approaching with palm fronds) Thought the pup might want a better shelter.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Where'd you come from? (turning) I was just thinking the same thing. Like, I've been sitting here worrying about it...

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sits in the rain under the palms. It's fine there, but it could be drier. (sets down fronds, sits)

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Right. Except I don't think I was really worrying about the shelter. (laughs at himself) I was waiting for something to be wrong so I could fix it.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: (stands, shakes, presses against Marcus's leg)

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're fine, aren't you. (scratches the pup's ears)

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: (tail thumps, moves to sniff Ryan, then back to Marcus, circling both)

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It loves you more. Look at that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's safe with me. That's all.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: No, that's not true. It loves you more. I see it every day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It loves you the same. You just want it to choose you specifically.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. Yeah, I do. (defensive, then softer) Because I need to be chosen. Every time. Even by a dog.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You are chosen. By me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even with Jade and Judy in the picture?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: (finally looks at him) Even with them. I chose you. That didn't change when I chose them.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I keep thinking I'm secondary.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: (settles between them both, pressing into their legs, exhales)

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay, I believe you. (shoulders drop)

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We can build the pup a better shelter. Keep it drier in the rains.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. Let's do that. (stands, extends his hand to Marcus) Come on.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: (takes his hand, stands) After we gather the leaves.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: (follows both of them as they stand, tail up, no anxiety)

action: Settles on a log in the garden with the stray beside him, watching Ryan and Jade work with quiet attention.
thought: They're all here. That's still surprising me.

action: Kneels in the garden with Jade, measuring and testing the weathered garden posts, voice warm and steady.
thought: Fixing things. Being here. This is enough.

action: Works beside Ryan in the garden, crouching and pushing on the wooden posts to assess the damage.
thought: I'm not running. I'm actually staying.

action: Emerges from the house and settles on a stone in the garden, watching the others work, her deepest fear surfacing.
thought: What if they leave? I couldn't survive.

action: Lies down among them as they work, head resting between their feet, tail thumping in response to the emotional moment.
thought: Safe. All here. All safe together.

description: In the garden at midday, Ryan and Jade repair posts while Marcus arrives with the stray; when Judy joins, they talk about staying—about fear, about choosing to believe.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The base is soft. That rain two days ago soaked deeper than I thought.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I noticed yesterday. Wasn't sure if I should worry you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Worry me. That's what we do now. For each other.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What's happening?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Just maintenance. Garden's taking damage slower than I expected, actually.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Good.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You could help us, if you want.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm already here.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You know what I was thinking about?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How we used to be careful around each other. All of us. And now we're just—fixing things together like it's the easiest thing we've ever done.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It is easy. Being with you all is easy.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is the garden falling apart? Should I be worried?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: No, it's fine. Just keeping it solid.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'll stay out here then. I get tired of being inside.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're past the worst of it now, I think.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm terrified that won't last. That one day you'll all decide—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We won't. You know we won't.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I know. But I'm scared to believe it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Believe it. Please just believe it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tonight we can say it all again if you need us to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We'll say it as many times as it takes.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You'd do that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yes.

action: Holds the stray close as evening settles, letting it rest fully against his chest while he listens.
thought: They're all here. That still surprises me.

action: Sits by the fire, voice steady as he shares the fear he wakes with every morning.
thought: I can finally believe they're choosing me.

action: Settles close to Ryan, listening and affirming each person as they speak what's been quietly held.
thought: This is what staying actually feels like.

action: Sits across the fire, one hand on her belly, finally voicing the terror she's been carrying about the baby.
thought: They won't leave me. I'm starting to know.

action: Rests its head fully on Marcus's knee, breathing deeply, utterly at peace for the first time.
thought: Safe. Held. Never alone. Always home.

description: As evening settles, they gather at the fire and finally voice their fears—Judy about the baby, Ryan about belonging, Marcus about staying—while the stray sleeps trust into Marcus's lap.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I realized something today.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're all still terrified. But differently.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: How do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Before it was alone. Separately terrified. Now it's shared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that better?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It changes what fear means. Makes it bearable.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I wake up afraid too.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About what?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The baby. Everything coming. What I can't protect.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
